inclusion_criteria: Human studies conducted in the ASEAN region (Vietnam, Cambodia, Laos PDR, Thailand, Myanmar, Malaysia, Indonesia, Singapore, Philippines, Timor-Leste/East Timor, Brunei) involving people living or working there
Evaluate exposure to agricultural land-use (living or working in agricultural areas) with a comparator group unexposed to agricultural land-use (non-agricultural residents/workers)

Report infectious disease prevalence or seroprevalence in humans (e.g., hookworm, malaria, scrub typhus, spotted fever group rickettsioses, other human pathogen infections) with data enabling comparison by exposure status
Observational study designs with primary human data (cross-sectional, case-control, or cohort) providing extractable prevalence measures
exclusion_criteria: Not set in the ASEAN region or participants not residing/working in the listed ASEAN countries

No agricultural exposure contrast (e.g., only agricultural populations without a non-agricultural comparator, or exposure not defined); studies limited to vectors, animals, or environmental samples without human infection outcomes
Do not report infection prevalence/seroprevalence outcomes (e.g., incidence-only, mortality, clinical severity, knowledge/attitudes/practices without infection data)

Non-original or unsuitable designs for the question (reviews, editorials, modeling without primary human data, RCTs of clinical treatments, case reports/series, conference abstracts without sufficient extractable data)
screening_criteria.description: Screen studies assessing the association between agricultural land-use exposure and infectious disease prevalence among human populations in Southeast Asia, including only observational human studies that compare exposed versus unexposed groups.
